query: South Korea KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell value weekly latest June 2026, recent trend in trillion won
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on KOSPI foreign investor net buy/sell activity:

**Latest data (July 2026):**
- **July 8:** net BUY +343.6 bn won → **July 9:** net BUY +134.8 bn won → **July 10:** net SELL −322.56 bn won
- Foreigners ran a 13-session selling streak (June 19–July 7), briefly bought July 8–9, then reverted to selling July 10
- KOSPI closed 7,475.94 on July 10 (+2.52%)

**Dominant trend — record H1 2026 outflow:**
- **~148.3 trillion won** (~$95B) net foreign selling in H1 2026 — largest first-half outflow on record
- **157.3 trillion won** ($102.5B) cumulative YTD through July 3
- Selling ~90% concentrated in Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix

**June 2026 inflection:**
- Selloff began June 3; -1.24 tn won on June 8; brief mid-June reversals (~1 tn won buying June 15, a ~2 tn won buying week after a 35-day absence)

**Notable paradoxes:** Despite record outflows, KOSPI rose ~91.9% YTD and foreign ownership % actually *rose* from 36.65% to 40.47% (price appreciation). KRX framed it as rebalancing, not lost confidence; drivers include won weakness and rotation into Japan. All figures trace to Korea Exchange data via CNBC, Korea JoongAng Daily, Korea Times, Seoul Economic Daily, and Bloomingbit.

sources.title: Foreign investors' record Kospi sell-off may continue in second half as won weakens
sources.snippet: A record 148 trillion won ($95 billion) in net selling by foreign investors during the first half; net sales of 148.32 trillion won in the Kospi market.

sources.title: Foreign investors may have more Korean stocks to sell
sources.snippet: Foreign investors sold 157.3 trillion won ($102.5 billion) of shares on the KOSPI this year through July 3; index surged 91.9 percent.

sources.title: Kospi, SK Hynix, Samsung Electronics: Why foreign investors are selling
sources.snippet: Overseas investors had unloaded a net 1.24 trillion won (about $801 million) worth of Kospi-listed shares.

sources.title: Kospi jumps 2.52% as chip stocks rally; Korean won strengthens

sources.snippet: Foreigners returned after 35 days, net buyers of about 2 trillion won; Kospi up 4.63% restoring 8,000 threshold.

sources.title: Kospi selloff is rebalancing, not vote against South Korea: KRX CEO
sources.snippet: KRX CEO says selloff is portfolio rebalancing; Kospi grew 76% in 2025 and was up 108.85% YTD before selloff began June 3.

sources.title: Foreign Investors Absorb Retail Selloff as KOSPI Sees 1 Trillion Won Net Buying
sources.snippet: KOSPI reclaimed 8,500 with about 1 trillion won foreign net buying absorbing retail selloff.

sources.title: Foreign investors dump Korean stocks at record pace as money flows to Japan
